textByHashes.1adf99d7a298ac27d9265b8b1b694b5372f47c32: Ah , glad to see you made it. Welcome to Lletya, home to the Elven Resistance.
textByHashes.beadb97487ae18b9a741b18b4937e68d1e033723: That's not surprising, he is not particularly fond of humans. But if you've earnt his trust, it proves to me that I was right to take a chance on you.
textByHashes.a0f8ee38bed6298d7fadca13995d514ae2096233: Well you're here now and you still have a chance to right your previous wrongs.
textByHashes.a0cae127ad51bb1316686263069d2372e0a5ff63: I'm afraid that a lot is still unknown to us as well. However, I will explain what I can. Be aware that this is only a brief overview. You'll find some books around the village that explain more if you wish though.
textByHashes.f47fdaa044944065c8241298cabe528e9f6ee065: Many years ago, all elves lived in harmony within the crystal city of Prifddinas alongside our goddess, Seren. When the God Wars ended, Seren was lost to us. For the first time, we were alone. To lead us through those difficult times, Baxtorian Cadarn was named the first king of the elves. Under Baxtorian, our kingdom expanded into the east. We prospered for a time, but little did we know that an enemy walked among us. During that time, Lord Iorwerth turned away from Seren and pledged himself to Zamorak, the Dark Lord. A larger kingdom meant our forces were further spread. Lord Iorwerth took advantage of that. He claimed Prifddinas as his own. Baxtorian tried to take it back from him, but Prifddinas was built to survive the God Wars. There was no way to retake it. In a last attempt to keep the city safe, Baxtorian and the clan elders sung the song of inversion which reverted the entire city into a crystal seed.
textByHashes.05e3c0d1abc654b05e1bf0ff811ad9ea18bc668a: Indeed. After that, Baxtorian returned to the east but found that the rest of the kingdom had fallen while he was away. Not only that, but his wife, Glarial, had been taken.
textByHashes.9549673dd5d6110bf61fcc28585335ca5fbf2269: That's good to hear, I can see why you managed to earn Islwyn's trust. Anyway, with Baxtorian gone and Prifddinas lost, our land erupted into a Civil War. We've been fighting ever since.
textByHashes.897ed838f402a518421ad72e4eb2d23cdb5e7c89: That is the bit we're not so sure on. We know little about the lands to the east. From what we can tell, it seems that Lord Iorwerth has promised to support King Lathas in reclaiming some land his family once owned.
textByHashes.802fcbb5c1ea820a5bffec042b66e9415825b3d5: That's what we want you to find out. According to our spies, Lord Iorwerth has used the alliance with King Lathas as an opportunity to infiltrate West Ardougne. The city must contain something he needs.
textByHashes.f42b83263a8363871966d2f427988825940453b3: Those that you know as mourners are in fact elves in service to Lord Iorwerth. We regularly see them crossing the Arandar mountain pass and into the east.
textByHashes.ce15998a8f2df290c296bcec6db9a49f7c316713: That they are. We assume that the plague is a cover up for them to enter the city unchallenged. We don't know why though. That's why we need you. You know the city and can freely travel there. You are the perfect person for this task. We need you to go to West Ardougne, infiltrate the mourners and find out what they are doing in the city.
textByHashes.213190b90c0ae6570d008ae4286fe6d381e3a7d3: I'd argue that understanding is essential to what comes next. But as you wish.
textByHashes.f0b4d7ca41114bf684d7400d6297ab796101bb8e: The bit that is relevant to you is the recent alliance between King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth.
textByHashes.6b2785ff221a984579c71058ead448576bf427e0: From what we can tell, it seems that Lord Iorwerth has promised to support King Lathas in reclaiming some land his family once owned.
textByHashes.17e262f58af3dedee185467254412b14327ac40c: That they are. We assume that the plague is a cover up for them to enter the city unchallenged. We don't know why though. That's why we need you.
textByHashes.0d25fbb3040d54f43e8570dd00c79de227622dbe: You know the city and can freely travel there. You are the perfect person for this task. We need you to go to West Ardougne, infiltrate the mourners and find out what they are doing in the city.
textByHashes.97c904cf31eec054dce5877bc1fef2ef34ab2ab9: I don't have an exact plan for you I'm afraid. You know the city better than any of us so are best placed to make a plan yourself. However as I said before, there have been sightings of mourners crossing the Arandar mountain pass. I'm sure you can take advantage of that.
textByHashes.8ee9a6cc5d762eb21d4df5e2b040dcc3e8e8f2ac: Hmm... Well you can try asking our local seamstress, Oronwen, about the trousers. As for the top, you'll need to find a way to get it cleaned. Maybe you can find someone who does a lot of laundry. Someone who wears lots of white might be a good bet.
textByHashes.1813ffa5b56c2985c55848041ef1c01eb778514a: Ah, a disguise. Good thinking. Use it to enter the Mourner Headquarters in West Ardougne. Once your in, see if you can find out what they are doing in the city.
textByHashes.1b31f2bb16e25b9b7c8d7bed9a69afe134632c0c: Temple? That must be the Temple of Light!
textByHashes.1c5d069133bf05f97f348cb8dafbccfcfd280750: It was built by Seren herself to guard a dark and ancient power. This is not good friend, I fear Lord Iorwerth intends to use that power to summon the Dark Lord!
textByHashes.5539d9cf3ef4afa4b65a0c0bc6aa23d2f562f133: Well then we still have time. Thank you for your help so far, we would never have discovered this without you. Now we must prepare for what comes next.
textByHashes.80f2aa83e6d62ef2ae9f0c09eeb9d1cb01405e16: First of all, we need to know how close the Iorwerth elves are to finding it. Do you think you are ready for this?
textByHashes.732084f2c2e2c39670ea99195faf2d1a4c68d6b9: Well don't take too long. Every second we waste is a big risk.
textByHashes.00a8d2945aebfcb744d7a48aa153e96f3b3deedc: First of all, we need to know how close the Iorwerth elves are to finding it. Return to the Mourner Headquarters and gain access to their mine. Once there, see what you can discover about the temple.
textByHashes.dec9c15a540e1bca77a117b4a1d6bc4d453c1652: Thank you friend. Don't forget your disguise.
textByHashes.eb514b6d6da34d1c0df5dd4ba44c38e6e874ea71: This isn't good, it will only be a matter of time before they discover the temple.
textByHashes.2a3c97d74d13e49661c49f6386710be777d7c314: I did some research while you were gone. Apparently, the temple was built with safeguards to prevent all but Seren's chosen from entering.
textByHashes.de86b36123cf63e6839f7227ec33141814ae4dff: If there are shadows roaming outside the temple, it means those safeguards have long since decayed. I believe we can still restore them though.
textByHashes.9459943a0f63eb48ff20693c00259024de17057a: The temple guards an ancient altar, one with powers over life and death. I suspect this altar is why Lord Iorwerth is so keen to access the temple.
textByHashes.733ed6c0c5a5c93a7084d2bb79ce1f726e4f1725: Indeed. Now, the altar is also key to the safeguards on the temple. We will need to use it to restore them.
textByHashes.cb38f2e25b524e1f28659dcd335057185d8af5d7: First, I need you to return to the temple and find the crystal that is powering the safeguards. As they are no long working, I expect the crystal will be black in colour.
textByHashes.6524bba07115faa2cbe5b5564c6892ba29dd78a3: Bring me a sample of this crystal. We will then determine our next steps.
textByHashes.5345fff7b6dc7167ab954cad327b5093a497f35e: Well done, with this sample, a good crystal chanter should be able to make a replacement. Just a second, I will summon Eluned.
textByHashes.d78cc0877aa9b7cabe920446b44040476d8e1210: Thank you for responding to my summons Eluned.
textByHashes.a8ff11ede1a19c7be7f82e55702436c68bca0df0: here has an old crystal sample, we need you to make a replacement for it.
textByHashes.8e33888de03db3fa66870ac339cc26d743f0ba87: You will need to first take the newly formed crystal to the end of the temple and place it on the altar there. This will imbue the crystal with the power it needs to safeguard the temple.
textByHashes.c134b375a75d06775143822fd8d210b7dde927aa: It should be quite simple. Once you have powered up the newly formed crystal, you will need to return to the blackened crystal and place the new shard with others. This should restore the safeguards.
textByHashes.18cbcae0ce6e77c0af1c9c268d222eefbfb7b6c3: This is good news indeed, . That should stop anyone from entering for another thousand years.
textByHashes.9200359fb946ede98d9da8045075281478de92bc: This is interesting news. I will send an envoy down to speak with them. We must ensure all access into the temple is secure.
textByHashes.f0f3e090f1f4e36cb5dd82396e6e0a533b1593f9: Even still, I can't see Lord Iorwerth discovering this tunnel any time soon. For now, he should have no way to summon the Dark Lord.
textByHashes.a9dc90b0b879cc90d8a17573a8332ad804ecb6d0: This isn't the end by any means. The Iorwerth forces are still at large and we are not strong enough to stop them. However, with the temple secure, the main threat has been dealt with.
textByHashes.093aa8f53530ec275137a6eee13ebf482e611c77: I will let you know if there are any further developments. For now though, your work is done.
textByHashes.5b08c58bf9a71a500caa86baf4f51d28e785b105: I can't offer much of a reward I'm afraid. However, I did have Eluned make this for you. It should allow you to bypass the defences on the temple.
textByHashes.252881af3abf870220971b658cb6168e46ab5441: Are you the human by the name ?
textByHashes.1e22929ddd30c2091e0cdaa82f227e762ef7ac5d: Thank Seren, maybe all is not yet lost.
textByHashes.5a723daec5b046c3b139d8c56c93dbdb2f285958: I am Arianwyn, leader of the Elven Resistance. We've been watching you since you arrived in Tirannwn. We know it was you that murdered King Tyras.
textByHashes.e862a442261fcb4aace201639c4993dca9e01b00: There is much to explain and little time. You carry a message from Lord Iorwerth, do you not?
textByHashes.e3cfc8f9e0abcf9a7c3d7a61d9320e32ff4c9d59: Good, we understand each other well. Now hand it here and find the truth of who your real employer is.
textByHashes.e31f47242afd8238aa96e792c3b1b603e9834792: I have a few ideas, now is not the time to discuss them though. For now, you should go to King Lathas as normal. Give him the message and act like nothing unusual has happened.
textByHashes.f23f4d54d279fe0c95fadef296b2d4331039d643: Once you are done, return to Tirannwn and seek out Islwyn and Eluned. You'll find them roaming the Isafdar forest. They will bring you to me.
textByHashes.646e19e2fbf5804153a0d5e532812305a628d7f0: The chance for redemption after all you've done? If that doesn't take your fancy, consider this...
textByHashes.e9d2c9c7a2a820d17febc814c6188bc41c61e25e: This isn't a struggle for land or title. It is a war for life, all life. If Iorwerth and Lathas manage to summon Zamorak, we all die.
textByHashes.4199a2ecfdc8e77e4dabb1225a5e5684bbef2063: Deliver your message, act normal. We'll meet again soon.
textByHashes.c2316b62d9d9ddd5d2078d6ce937c85df317dacb: That's enough Lord Iorwerth, let them go!
textByHashes.947333b4c932e6f93241915bb5fbe87ef63f3973: Don't be so sure. We fight with the strength of ten of your warriors.
textByHashes.20c5c99cbed40eb53cc1b564dbbe4aef44d88be5: We don't have anything to discuss with you, you Zamorakian scum!
textByHashes.68294ef8137777362ab7371c7f29fc2e03a6ba4d: Don't try to manipulate us with your lies. We know all about your plot with King Lathas to summon the Dark Lord.
textByHashes.ab8821c4644ec9ae336185742522cb4c4b0442aa: You're lying! You wanted to use the Temple of Light to summon Zamorak!
textByHashes.de7133beeea8a3f034964a7c1af24cb8e05c64ec: Ah , there you are. We thought it best to let you rest for a bit after everything that happened earlier.
textByHashes.dd1185f3003cc108d7042f8706f2aca7fcbf8f07: I suspect you wouldn't have been around to have this conversation. You played a dangerous game in Ardougne. I fear you may not have considered the outcome of that.
textByHashes.4589a4f13915a38fd8c48b5f48e1d1578473b936: Of course not, but we have no idea how Lord Iorwerth will respond to this. The things he said when we freed you were... concerning...
textByHashes.7f2f7bcfd8d2a74e19aa68252b1d5f60356acb3d: I'm not sure. It's something that we should discuss with the others.
textByHashes.e12b3e5bb5ca7204802f043052e221c79afa5222: Could you do me a favour and fetch Ysgawyn. He's probably on the upper platforms. You should bring your friend Elena as well. I'm not sure where she is I'm afraid, Ysgawyn might know though.
textByHashes.c7fdd8729a3aabfa0ca4aba6e4505c5d203b363a: Right, now that we're all here, let's get started.
textByHashes.36a7bb877cf42d62939090a8c97a6560a4d711ca: So, as I'm sure you've all heard, King Lathas of Ardougne is dead. He was overthrown by his subjects and executed soon afterwards.
textByHashes.f580211913d4ddadecb585a4580b37eeb2680dbf: That's not all though, the mourners of West Ardougne have been driven out of the city. Lord Iorwerth no longer has any presence in Ardougne.
textByHashes.9a6648a72e409084a1d529e5d903d0f67f8df64b: That's enough Ysgawyn. We can debate what's happened for days and still reach no conclusion. That is not why I called you all here. Instead, we need to plan what happens next.
textByHashes.df21c54721999b4aa8530965594ec47769b0c6b5: Now, while freeing and Elena from the Iorwerth camp, we were confronted by Lord Iorwerth. He could have killed us all, but he let us go.
textByHashes.bbfe44c38aa3f46907a8534c643a783e660b9ad5: His reasoning was disturbing. For a long time now, we've believed that Lord Iorwerth has been trying to summon Zamorak, the Dark Lord. However, he claimed otherwise.
textByHashes.e94db87818b6159bfd214e3fbf74c11c8e95d5db: He seems to think otherwise. He claimed that a part of Seren still lives, trapped within the Temple of Light. Apparently, he discovered this by breaking one of the nine seals within the Grand Library.
textByHashes.e5de124a0126e23a664d807bfb689896d74a841f: As for the Grand Library, it was a great archive of knowledge beneath Prifddinas. Pretty much everything there was open to all of elven kind.
textByHashes.a5100933d37a0ba2aea769b8d536eaffafe903d5: There was an exception to that though, the nine seals of Seren. The seals guarded the forbidden knowledge, that which only Seren herself could access.
textByHashes.70e8dd376a99370370d616580f680debf7683e2d: Their plan worked, and the city was lost to Lord Iorwerth. However, it was lost to the rest of us as well, the Grand Library with it.
textByHashes.bcaed274e9ecc953a8d940cdb02b15c6f0a9ae75: We can't possibly make a plan without knowing Lord Iorwerth's intentions. While unlikely to be true, this stuff about the library is all we have to go on.
textByHashes.47639ca89c638fd912222d1c889b49dc87be03fd: If Baxtorian left behind anything that could help us, we should try to find it.
textByHashes.6c30587e67ebe7d94d5788fa406b013b6ec11b77: , you've been to Baxtorian Falls multiple times. You're the best person to take the lead on this.
textByHashes.ce771a08050c600a2cbe78c9c35a5976bf4460e5: I'd like to accompany you on this one , it might be useful to have an elf with you.
textByHashes.b0514603970818317e013f37208b2b74a1d05e67: Thank you Islwyn. While we're gone, the rest of you should focus on Lord Iorwerth. See if you can get any idea on what he's planning now that he's lost Ardougne. Just be careful.
textByHashes.4b1badaf2db10793cdea0d51605a8ec467ac0576: Thank you for agreeing to help us, . We owe a lot to you, even if some of us don't show it very well.
textByHashes.2b029330fa4fe96988642e4881dc383e5eace951: Try not to be too judgemental. Ysgawyn's husband was killed by Essyllt, one of Lord Iorwerth's henchmen. Ysgawyn's never really been the same since.
textByHashes.b590e004a7ca638cccb3a91167190e77c5074140: He's a nasty piece of work. If you try hard enough, you can find something redeemable about most elves, even those that serve Lord Iorwerth.
textByHashes.4fb39be703daa5523880661d94a3e0ee5046273b: But Essyllt... He seems to take joy in the suffering of others, especially those unable to defend themselves.
textByHashes.449445669133e1fcd8474f52a5d16e499c3aa72e: Enough about him though, we need to focus on Baxtorian Falls. I suggest we travel seperately, we can't be too careful at the moment.
textByHashes.899b6eccc6909fbd81a8ce3cb6a7cc9cd03fc4aa: I'm no expert on your lands but I should be fine. I just have a few things to gather before I depart.
textByHashes.2742962e73aa868e1927b3de567248cce8485d78: I just have a few things to gather before I depart for Baxtorian Falls. I'll meet you there.
textByHashes.58f684e41077d8ebfd1b257c68328ac32dee1908: Hello there , glad to see you made it here okay.
textByHashes.32b3361be0049d1bbf4e8269ecab3a53560e1e06: I must say, it feels strange to be back here, especially after all of this time.
textByHashes.67d397dcd43c0dfaa8d2174e55faaeed78d2da7d: I was one of the elves born in Baxtorian's new kingdom. My family were close relations of Baxtorian, he invited us here a few times.
textByHashes.bdc953b0c0ee16b8d0c3b4f18cdc3d3ec794b050: Those were simpler times, happier times. After Baxtorian left to try and retake Prifddinas, my parents were among those who joined him. They never returned. I was still just a child at the time, alone and afraid.
textByHashes.da783c5af7a3c71455f68416fd08677d93a09878: This war has taken so much... From my people, and from yours. I'd give anything to see peace, anything to finally see Prifddinas with my own eyes.
textByHashes.da1ad311a28647edfd0e8bdf4b208a7130c758cc: Sorry, now's not the time for this. We need to see if Baxtorian left anything of use behind.
textByHashes.abf78ac40aa50a2607d1fa7c1642b863e9ed94f7: Here's that book that Islwyn gave us. Hopefully it has something of use in it.
textByHashes.c884761bc4685c278a046c660e47706a4b91066b: To be honest... I really don't know. It seems impossible, and yet...
textByHashes.df7e326d506ba2509dedaff4b485ceaa09a0c0fa: No. It's just me being wishful. He's lying, he has to be.
textByHashes.532610d9835bffd01ac4acd0c857ae6f8f960757: By the time I was born, Seren was already gone from this world. All I know of her is the stories my parents told me.
textByHashes.1eb3864b9d5a3cf3a622caabba5b4addb9ed69e0: I do, but that doesn't mean I should fall for the lies of Lord Iorwerth. Seren is gone, nothing can ever bring her back.
textByHashes.1e61cd80c9a617bc3e5e180b4abf2048b598b2b3: I won't lie, we're clutching straws here. We have nothing else to go on though, so we have to try.
textByHashes.1e6ba0153363c3a140132af629e62b3bb658ad02: Baxtorian was once king of the elves, anything he left behind might be of use.
textByHashes.6622a5448f16e993c1ed1f48f119c34b5111c9c0: We need to see if Baxtorian left anything of use behind. Maybe that book will be of use.
textByHashes.8a98d84c8ceb6e417528323a6aa14478834db289: If it's any consolation, moved her remains here, to be closer to you.
textByHashes.43a72ff10ca00811a886f1280bb79c92972bce65: Then I can take you back to Lletya. Your grandson, Islwyn, is there.
textByHashes.ee633434ec53b7a3482cb679d25ab65fb2938cdc: Very well, I will teleport us back to Lletya. You'll have to make your own way back I'm afraid, .
textByHashes.6a23255b8a61f1c4c06d445404f43c9e20c56084: Welcome back . While you were gone, I filled Baxtorian in on everything that has happened. I also took the liberty of calling the others here, so that we can discuss our next move.
textByHashes.cc9cf3047202212f4c698bb39b27d6d077e437eb: I appreciate we're all happy to have Baxtorian back with us, but let's not get distracted.
textByHashes.01e815f7f40df5b636bf07d5c0beb0a4c429554a: You don't mean entering the crystal do you?
textByHashes.5255a1b2f4aaac6b687ea9060aa267f664c306df: Perfect. Elena, if you could help create this potion, that would be greatly appreciated. The rest of us can work on those defences.
textByHashes.21fcc25c0b60681d2da5cab7fa6da5478cfad085: Baxtorian has given Elena the information she needs to make this potion. You should speak to her by the pond.
textByHashes.7d0bfb1e0e0c0beefe88a32f865ee6846500b1db: I suspect there is. Myself and Baxtorian have been looking over how we can get past the defences around the crystal seed.
textByHashes.8c5bb3d7bb7f3dfbf80eb5828c82042e1c54215b: With Prifddinas gone, Lord Iorwerth has claimed the land where it once was. He's managed to manipulate the defences to only allow his allies to enter.
textByHashes.72f873232e9270c986fde8ed88ae0e5680a7ce9a: There were eight separate elven clans that journeyed with Seren to this world. Those eight clans still exist today, with each elf born into one of them.
textByHashes.adea29e1e24a9dc3e8d83a54f614e6fcaf79ff1c: You'll have already heard of some of the clans. The Iorwerth clan is known to you, the traitorous clan of assassins and slayers.
textByHashes.8bcdde50655086077c1670b648ac5fc9f913ee0c: The other clans you might be less familiar with. However, one of those clans is key here, the Amlodd clan, the clan of mystics.
textByHashes.f8a0a3bd6ed8ea0247e92b2206ecb6c90a59756b: Lord Amlodd may be our priority, but the other elders could be of great help in our fight against Lord Iorwerth. We should try and find them all.
textByHashes.32fdfec37b8a32cf6fc6d62087b15514886132e6: , myself and Baxtorian will continue to work on deciphering these clues. While we do, it would be of great help if you could find Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn for us.
textByHashes.21a7e8abc12f8f3b2ca4c00fe7d5a25ee307c731: We need you to find Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn for us. Speak to Baxtorian, he can tell you about the clues they left behind.
textByHashes.578f0fe444bc5f6bf5cba97659fb246975798dc7: Good job finding Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn. Now, you should probably go see how that seed you planted is doing.
textByHashes.8bfd0663364da531425e70143f634e64de344280: Crystal dust? Interesting. Well I have an old crystal here that you can use. It will need grinding up though.
textByHashes.e1eea37ab0b1bd133674de0048d3b9bd4a9217b0: Why don't you try using that crystal I gave you?
textByHashes.151d52b2245265476850876eb6ab2adf68d19257: Great, I'll look after it for you. Now, let's call the others and discuss our plan for the Grand Library.
textByHashes.50ae823cd9e2c969e9fe66aa91ab1bbf073272a7: Right, thanks to , we should now have everything we need to enter the crystal seed containing Prifddinas.
textByHashes.df1ae022f1a0c2a7399e5fc4a59c6753be11bad8: Indeed we do. Baxtorian's knowledge allowed to reunite us with Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn. It was the Amlodd clan that created those defences, Lord Amlodd can overcome them for us.
textByHashes.d9337be30a9e462cc8dc5872647fd90a3584e132: Then I propose that be the one sent into the crystal. Anyone disagree?
textByHashes.ecad50cfe7fbd73c02fa7254a874b6de1c9b8b13: Yes we already know you do Ysgawyn. Anyone else?
textByHashes.1a3ec7b2f985da74cb0967d3d8f954f722b118d5: Right, , talk to Lord Amlodd when you are ready. Good luck.
textByHashes.f14973a122d888bf9525aa58188c8096cd28a528: Good luck in the library, . We're all counting on you. Speak to Lord Amlodd when you're ready.
textByHashes.30db138867c15183f680cc571a6d9aedb11d188f: , you have returned. Did you discover anything in the Grand Library?
textByHashes.8f54511b257489719465222200d32642a66dbc73: I see. I will summon them at once.
textByHashes.c9a4af895708ea7e1c22683277f57ea753603500: Okay, thank you all for joining us. In case you weren't aware, here has returned from the Grand Library with some news for us.
textByHashes.93b16c511e12be9788b6932a897d39a6f4db34e5: So you managed to break all of Seren's seals? Very impressive . Now tell us, this memory, what was it?
textByHashes.6911372b95c57e7bd7277197658bc76a126f4e02: If... If what you say is true, how does it relate to Lord Iorwerth's claims?
textByHashes.82da07ff38e2c5440cb5c14c4d445fe95fb0c2f2: I don't know what to do!
textByHashes.cbacd7c78bc9be83d06c0770b2c7a260173715a0: I don't know what to do...
textByHashes.4b0c9f987a8c2bcb7513206f8e3e6c1737dbe400: You think I know something you all don't? You think I have some trick hidden up my sleeve? I have nothing!
textByHashes.1079c48c204fa515a8eab472df933327e343803d: And how would you have me do that, Ysgawyn? How am I meant to respond to this?
textByHashes.c2ed267ffa7ee826f2fd87506c895105e6f76390: Am I? My whole life, I've been fighting Lord Iorwerth. My whole life, I've believed him to be a servant of Zamorak. Now I learn that everything I fought for was a lie.
textByHashes.f7b88f4357e5d58f5718132700902842ea40b266: I don't even know what I'm leading any more...
textByHashes.f9308453e9edc8fb37f9054af5e91563659198b7: I need time alone... Time to think... Baxtorian, I would appreciate it if you could take charge for a while.
textByHashes.89ccf0bb5dfc952938ab91e51472e928a3c4f8c8: That's all I ask. Now, I would like to be alone.
textByHashes.6ba1730da6a1738683536666398e7ded91c16396: They're not the enemy. I thought they were, for so long, but they're not.
textByHashes.5fd009cd2c69d734d3e902bc0156d6994d2f6144: I don't condone what he did, what any of them have done. But they want what we want, we have to try working together.
textByHashes.aeb4e8ad0275d3edb057ccdc760e073f1f08b013: Baxtorian, please. Just give him the orb.
textByHashes.5c024ccbc82fb8cb5fe066e754738c1561483ac6: It wasn't meant to go like this. No one was meant to get hurt. I'm sorry.
textByHashes.84b55adc1242e855f9d55fc085ac6307b314314d: For so long I was led to believe that Lord Iorwerth was our enemy. I thought he'd turned his back on Seren, thought he'd betrayed her.
textByHashes.d9cc95087101772763415ef97d97c788583dfb59: But I was wrong. Everything we thought we were fighting for was a lie. He never betrayed her, everything he did was to free her.
textByHashes.8516024c9d5c92871d95b3d0436ba36cbb74fe92: How do you know? Because of what you found in the library? How do we know that was her? How do we know what's true anymore?
textByHashes.1283b4d7651ea5b8533beda6ecccb62ea0338733: My whole life I've dreamed of her, dreamed of what it would be like to meet her. If there's even a chance that she's still alive, I have to know.
textByHashes.aab3f1aad0d8b6b6d1438de1340bf48b7a7fa4a7: Seren guided the elves for thousands of years. With her back, we'll finally be able to reform Prifddinas. We'll finally have peace again.
textByHashes.b0e48ca944da945bed3c6b06606d166f39144817: You... you know I can't do that.
textByHashes.98f2631672e41dacf17441b825400f5b2db71ddc: I'm sorry friend, but I've made my choice. Now please, walk away. No one else needs to die here.
textByHashes.5e3555d6979d52e7abf1a44977869dcb732ed4b4: And I can't let you go after him.
textByHashes.8544f25c2d2847b6467160e20e479a18e27a2526: That... that's not what we agreed.
textByHashes.f9d944323dcbdb9376e639cc5c3e5d3c7101d4a0: We agreed to enter the temple and free Seren. If that's not Seren in there, we shouldn't free her.
textByHashes.b75dc3e62b77b6bc60ec913ff5cd1ffe6b0377ae: I... I don't think it's her.
textByHashes.52a41f1512f84e95c3b7de8eb1d5079a2b8056a8: I may not have met our goddess, but I heard enough stories to know what she looked like. This thing, it's not her.
textByHashes.7cde135143e3fb6578ea00721a6d60c92b2228d8: Lord Iorwerth please, this isn't what we agreed.
textByHashes.176cbaf4bb2800ff5bbcfbcadf87f595cdd8e6e7: As well as can be expected. My guilt is a heavy load. A lot of people died because of me.
textByHashes.0a963818d6e9c689281705e388a500ea19ba7704: He wasn't evil, not truly. He just became so obsessed with his goal that he forgot why he started fighting in the first place.
textByHashes.8aa1b857996855fbd9d0d907e2685bade60b47ca: As for the fragment of Seren... I wish there had been another way.
textByHashes.fb3bf62cee0367488aa532967873304454e06937: No, and at least now her pain has ended. Still, I'll always wonder what could have been...
textByHashes.c051851eaa31f74b0dc02593aecec8fceaa666eb: There's no place for me here anymore, not after what I've done.
textByHashes.e7a8d456c02ee26474f5842e39a87b9305b0d195: I wanted to stay long enough to help repair the damage of my actions, and to look upon Prifddinas for the first time. I've done that now. Lletya has been rebuilt and Prifddinas has been reformed.
textByHashes.256f14978c650ffe3db121e5a62ba590f1d9c89b: I don't know. There's a lot of places on this world that I've never seen. Maybe one of them will help me find redemption, and forgiveness.
textByHashes.37c01e59ee6c9f6695484a58a5dfee7abbd5ef8f: The elves here will do fine. Baxtorian has his own path of redemption to go on, and I imagine he'll struggle far more than me. He has the other elders to support him though. Under them, our people will prosper.
textByHashes.660113184a2c61be46e4ebd34e4663d13331fe99: Anyway, you should get going. Baxtorian will be waiting for you.
textByHashes.f9013f070c5c7098a413250bf693cd41b1aa705d: How did you get in here? You must leave.
textByHashes.22f66cbd19ba887877b52a3b19999d1d1bc3a8dc: Not yet, I will try to send word if we find out anything new.
